zh.Bewsey Hall, Warrington, Lancashire, Eng: 英國藍卡爾貝福 霍爾
zh.Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾貝福 霍爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP於1254年出生在英國藍卡爾貝福 霍爾時，他的父親William le Boteler26歲，母親Dionysia Lostock20歲。他和Isabel le Boteler至少有5個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1297年12月在英國藍卡爾沃林頓享年43歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾沃林頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP于1254年出生在英国藍卡爾貝福 霍爾时，他的父亲William le Boteler26岁，母亲Dionysia Lostock20岁。他和Isabel le Boteler至少有5个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1297年12月在英国藍卡爾沃林頓享年43岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾沃林頓。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP was born in 1254, in Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England, his father, William le Boteler, was 26 and his mother, Dionysia Lostock, was 20. He had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Isabel le Boteler. He died in December 1297, in Warrington, Lancashire, England, at the age of 43, and was buried in Warrington, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP est né en 1254, à Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, William le Boteler, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Dionysia Lostock, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles avec Isabel le Boteler. Il est décédé en décembre 1297, à Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP 1254 in Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William le Boteler 26 und seine Mutter Dionysia Lostock 20. Er hatte mit Isabel le Boteler mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb im Dezember 1297 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Warrington, Lancashire, England und wurde in Warrington, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP è nato il nel 1254 nel Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, William le Boteler, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Dionysia Lostock, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie con Isabel le Boteler. Morì nel dicembre 1297, nel Warrington, Lancashire, England, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Warrington, Lancashire, England.

ja.Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアベウセイ ハル
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MPが1254年にイギリスランカシュアイアベウセイ ハルで生まれた時，父William le Botelerは26歳，母Dionysia Lostockは20歳であった。彼はIsabel le Botelerとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1297年12月にイギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンで43歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP(이)가 1254년 영국 랭카실르 브w시 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William le Boteler(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Dionysia Lostock(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Isabel le Boteler(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1297년 12월 영국 랭카실르 워링턴에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 워링턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP nasceu em 1254, em Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William le Boteler, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Dionysia Lostock, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas com Isabel le Boteler. Ele faleceu em dezembro de 1297, em Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP родился в 1254 г. в местности бюси холл, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени William le Boteler было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Dionysia Lostock, 20. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери от Isabel le Boteler. Он умер декабрь 1297 г. в местности варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Henry le Boteler - Lord of Warrington, MP nació en 1254, en Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, William le Boteler, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Dionysia Lostock, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas con Isabel le Boteler. Murió en diciembre de 1297, en Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
